days.title: Week 4, Day 1: Days of the Week

words.example_sentence_target: 月曜日に会議があります。
words.example_sentence_native: There is a meeting on Monday.

words.example_sentence_target: 火曜日はゴミの日です。
words.example_sentence_phonetic: Kayoubi wa gomi no hi desu.

words.example_sentence_target: 水曜日に映画を見に行きます。
words.example_sentence_native: I will go to see a movie on Wednesday.
words.example_sentence_phonetic: Suiyoubi ni eiga o mi ni ikimasu.

days.cultural_note: Japanese days of the week are named after celestial bodies and elements: Moon (getsu), Fire (ka), Water (sui), Wood (moku), Gold/Metal (kin), Earth (do), and Sun (nichi).
days.pronunciation_tip: The 'you' in each day is a long vowel: 'yoh'.

days.title: Week 4, Day 2: Days of the Week

words.example_sentence_target: 木曜日に図書館へ行きます。
words.example_sentence_native: I will go to the library on Thursday.

words.example_sentence_target: 金曜日の夜にパーティーがあります。
words.example_sentence_native: There is a party on Friday night.
words.example_sentence_phonetic: Kinyoubi no yoru ni paatii ga arimasu.

words.example_sentence_target: 日曜日に公園へ行きます。
words.example_sentence_native: I will go to the park on Sunday.

days.cultural_note: Most businesses are open on Saturdays, but many are closed on Sundays, similar to Western countries.
days.pronunciation_tip: Practice saying all seven days in order to get the rhythm.

days.title: Week 4, Day 3: Months of the Year

words.example_sentence_target: 四月に新学期が始まります。
words.example_sentence_native: The new school term starts in April.

days.cultural_note: Months in Japanese are very easy! It's just the number of the month followed by 'gatsu' (month).
days.pronunciation_tip: Note the irregular readings: April is 'shigatsu' (not yon-gatsu), July is 'shichigatsu', and September is 'kugatsu'.

days.title: Week 4, Day 4: Months of the Year

words.example_sentence_target: 五月にはゴールデンウィークがあります。
words.example_sentence_native: There is Golden Week in May.
words.example_sentence_phonetic: Gogatsu ni wa gooruden wiiku ga arimasu.

words.example_sentence_target: 六月は梅雨の季節です。
words.example_sentence_phonetic: Rokugatsu wa tsuyu no kisetsu desu.

words.example_sentence_target: 七月に夏休みが始まります。
八月はとても暑いです。

days.cultural_note: The Japanese school year starts in April, not September like in many Western countries.
days.pronunciation_tip: Practice the irregular month names: shigatsu (April), shichigatsu (July), kugatsu (September).

days.title: Week 4, Day 5: Months of the Year

words.example_sentence_target: 九月は秋の始まりです。
words.example_sentence_native: September is the beginning of autumn.
words.example_sentence_phonetic: Kugatsu wa aki no hajimari desu.

words.example_sentence_target: 十月には紅葉が美しいです。
words.example_sentence_native: The autumn leaves are beautiful in October.
words.example_sentence_phonetic: Juugatsu ni wa kouyou ga utsukushii desu.

words.example_sentence_target: 十一月はだんだん寒くなります。
words.example_sentence_native: It gradually gets colder in November.

words.example_sentence_target: 十二月にはクリスマスがあります。
words.example_sentence_phonetic: Juunigatsu ni wa kurisumasu ga arimasu.

days.cultural_note: Knowing the months is essential for making appointments and understanding holidays.
days.pronunciation_tip: Remember that 10 is 'juu', so October is 'juugatsu', not 'juu-gatsu'.

days.title: Week 4, Day 6: Final Review
days.title_native: 第四週、六日目：最終復習
days.cultural_note: Congratulations! You've learned the building blocks for all numbers, times, and dates in Japanese.
days.pronunciation_tip: Go back and listen to all the words from the Numbers path to refresh your memory.

days.title: Week 4, Day 7: Final Review
days.title_native: 第四週、七日目：最終復習
days.cultural_note: Try to spot numbers in Japanese media, like anime or news websites, to practice in a real-world context.
days.pronunciation_tip: Keep practicing! Consistency is the key to mastering Japanese numbers.
